//: Claude Code settings — deployed to ~/.config/ailocal/claude/settings.json
(CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for the local variant; ~/.claude — this cloud session — is never touched).
No secrets here. Base URL + API key are injected per-process by the
claude-local() wrapper (config/clients/configure.zsh), which reads them
from ~/.config/ailocal/env and sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
only for that one 'claude' invocation. Plain 'claude' in the same shell
is unaffected and stays on the cloud.
All requests route through LiteLLM using role-based model names.
Valid roles: coder-main | coder-agent | coder-fast | deep-think | deep-think-more | supervisor | embed
The 'model' field below is the LAUNCH default = coder-agent (qwen3.6), the
orchestrator role: a strong tool-caller that drives the session and delegates
planning to deep-think-more, edits to coder-main, review to supervisor. Drive
from a coder role, never from a reasoner/supervisor (those are subagents).
At runtime, /model lists every LiteLLM role — coder-main, coder-agent,
coder-fast, deep-think, deep-think-more, supervisor — because the claude-local
wrapper sets CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_GATEWAY_MODEL_DISCOVERY=1 (Claude Code then
GETs /v1/models from the proxy). The wrapper also remaps the built-in Opus/
Sonnet/Haiku slots to deep-think-more/coder-main/coder-fast so background
calls stay local. See config/clients/configure.zsh.
Launch with: claude-local
